blood_god_disciple.name: Blood God Disciple
description: ## Blood God Disciple
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/53›

A half-orc summoner who devotes himself to one of the bloody orc gods may believe his eidolon is an avatar of that god rather than a mere supernatural creature. A blood god disciple generally fights by the avatar's side and offers it blood sacrifices in exchange for martial prowess. A blood god disciple has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Half-orc}

::ab[Blood Feast (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost useMod=Charisma3 ability="A blood god disciple can feed a recently fallen foe to his eidolon, allowing the outsider to channel some of its power into the summoner. The eidolon must spend a @HLstandard_action to eat some of the opponent, which must be a living, corporeal creature killed or knocked unconscious by the eidolon or summoner in the past minute. This eating deals damage to the target as if the eidolon had attacked it with one natural attack (typically a bite). The fallen creature must have at least half as many Hit Dice as the summoner.~~~Once the feeding is complete, the summoner may manifest one 1-point evolution. This lasts for 1 minute. The ‹ability/evolution's› effects use the summoner's Hit Dice and ability scores rather than the eidolon's.~~~The blood god disciple may only apply one use of this ability at a time (using it a second time replaces any evolution manifested with this ability), and can only manifest evolutions his eidolon has." imp5="When the blood god disciple uses *blood feast,* he may manifest one 2-point evolution instead of a 1-point evolution." imp9="He may manifest up to two evolutions worth a total of 3 evolution points or fewer." imp13="He may manifest up to two evolutions worth a total of 4 evolution points or fewer." imp17="He may manifest up to three evolutions worth a total of 5 evolution points or fewer." replace="Summon monster I, III, V, VII, and IX"}

::ab[Bloody Gift (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="When a blood god disciple uses *blood feast* to manifest an evolution, he may touch one ally and grant it that evolution as well. Each affected ally counts as one use per day of the *blood feast* ability." imp7="The blood god disciple can affect an additional creature with this ability (each one counting as a use per day of *blood feast*)." repeat="He can affect an additional creature, following the rules above.~11~4" special="If the blood god disciple can manifest multiple evolutions per use of *blood feast,* his selected allies manifest these multiple evolutions as well." replace="Summon monster II"}

::ab[Avatar Gambit (Ex)]{l=7 head icon=power ability="When a blood god disciple dismisses his eidolon, he *rages* like a ‹class/barbarian› for a number of rounds equal to half his summoner level (he may end this *rage* early just like a barbarian, but if he does so, any remaining rounds of rage from this ability are lost)." replace="Summon monster IV"}

::ab[Rage (Ex)]{next icon=boost-down flavor="Relevant text from the barbarian ability." free="You can enter (or end) a *rage.* You gain a +4 morale bonus to your Strength and Constitution, as well as a +2 morale bonus on Will saves. The increase to Constitution grants you 2 hit points per Hit Dice, but these disappear when the *rage* ends and are not lost first like ‹rule/temporary hit points›." order=fp passive="While in *rage,* you take a -2 penalty to Armor Class, and you cannot use any Charisma-, Dexterity-, or Intelligence-based skills (except Acrobatics, Fly, Intimidate, and Ride) or any ability that requires patience or concentration. You are ‹misc/fatigued› after *rage* for a number of rounds equal to 2 times the number of rounds spent in the *rage.* You cannot enter a new *rage* while fatigued or ‹misc/exhausted› but can otherwise enter *rage* multiple times during a single encounter or combat. If you fall unconscious, your *rage* immediately ends, placing you in peril of death." special="Temporary increases to Constitution, such as those gained from *rage* and spells like ‹spell/bear's endurance›, do not increase the total number of rounds that a barbarian can *rage* per day. The total number of rounds of *rage* per day is renewed after resting for 8 hours, although these hours do not need to be consecutive."}

::ab[Rage Power (Ex)]{icon=power l11="A blood god disciple selects a barbarian ‹ability/rage power<s›, which he may use when *raging* (whether from the *avatar gambit* ability or actual barbarian *rage*)." l15="He selects a second rage power." replace="Summon monster VI and VIII spell-like"}

blood_summoner.name: Blood Summoner
description: ## Blood Summoner
‹SOURCE Champions of Corruption/24›

A blood summoner conjures an eidolon born from violence and blood. These cruel spellcasters are common among the god-callers of now-shattered Sarkoris, where the influence of the Worldwound has warped them into beings of chilling malevolence.
::cskill{gain=heal}

::ab[Blood Offering (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost standard="The blood summoner can sacrifice a flask of blood, extracted up to 1 day ago from a living creature or a corpse that's been dead no longer than 1 minute, to aid and appease an evil outsider within 30 feet. The offering grants the outsider a +2 enhancement bonus to the ability score of the blood summoner's choice and grants the blood summoner a +4 circumstance bonus on Diplomacy checks and Charisma checks to bargain with it." special="This blood can be extracted from a willing or helpless creature as a @HLfull-round_action that deals 1 point of ‹rule/Constitution damage›. The bonuses last for 10 minutes. The offering cannot be made to the same fiend twice in the same day." replace="Shield ally"}

::ab[Blood Travel (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic ability="A blood summoner can use his *maker's call* ability to allow his eidolon to travel through the blood of other creatures instead of bringing it to his side. The eidolon can either emerge from the blood of a living creature within range that has fewer than its maximum hit points remaining, or burst from the corpse of a Small or larger creature within range that has been dead for no longer than 1 minute. If the eidolon emerges from an injured creature, that creature takes 4d6 points of damage, which is halved if the creature succeeds at a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the blood summoner's level + his Charisma modifier). The eidolon appears in its choice of the nearest unoccupied square to the creature or corpse it emerged from, and is ‹misc/staggered› for 1 round." replace=Transposition}

::ab[Fiendish Calling (Su)]{icon=magic l10="The blood summoner's *summon monster* ability can additionally be used as ‹spell/lesser planar binding› as a @HLstandard_action, but only to call evil outsiders." l13="He can instead use it as ‹spell/planar binding›." l16="He can instead use it as ‹spell/greater planar binding›." replace="Greater shield ally"}

::ab[Blood Possession (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic useInc=summoner~2~16 useF="16~1~2" fullround="A blood summoner's eidolon can enter and control the body of any corporeal creature that has blood. This ability functions as ‹spell/marionette possession›, except that the eidolon's body also enters the target and the target can be unwilling. A successful Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the blood summoner's level + his Charisma modifier) negates this effect. The eidolon can end this possession at any time as a @HLstandard_action." special="If the host body is slain while the eidolon is possessing it, the eidolon is immediately ejected, takes 4d6 points of damage, and is ‹misc/stunned› for 1 round." replace="Merge forms"}

broodmaster.name: Broodmaster
description: ## Broodmaster
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/78›

Most summoners bond with one eidolon for their entire lives, but the broodmaster forges a link with multiple smaller eidolons that make up his brood. Separately, a broodmaster's eidolons are no match for a summoner's single eidolon, but taken as a whole, an eidolon brood makes up in quantity what it lacks in individual power.

::ab[Eidolon Brood]{l=2 icon=power ability="Instead of a single eidolon, a broodmaster summons two Small ‹sidekick/eidolon«s› to his side, each less powerful than the single eidolon of a standard summoner. Each eidolon has it own base form and associated base statistics and free evolutions. Regardless of the number of eidolons in the brood, each eidolon has the same base attack bonus and base saving throw bonuses, but the rest of the eidolons' base statistics must be divided between the eidolons, including Hit Dice (minimum 1), skill points, number of feats, armor bonus, Str/Dex bonus, evolution pool (but see below), and maximum number of natural attacks. Individual eidolons in the brood must purchase evolutions separately. Once a broodmaster decides on the forms and abilities of his eidolons, they cannot be changed until the summoner gains a level.~~~**Example:** A 2nd-level broodmaster can summon two eidolons. Each eidolon has 1 Hit Dice, BAB +2, +3 on its two ‹misc/good saves› and +0 on its bad save. The broodmaster decides to give the first eidolon 4 skill points, one feat, a +1 armor bonus, a +1 bonus to Strength, 2 points from the evolution pool, and a maximum of two attacks. The remaining 4 skill points, +1 armor bonus, +1 bonus to Dexterity, and 2 points from the evolution pool go to the second eidolon, but it gains no feats and can have only one natural attack." special="The broodmaster must dismiss all of his eidolons at one time. Likewise, all of the eidolons in a brood are banished if the broodmaster is unconscious, asleep, or killed. However, each eidolon in the brood is sent back to its home plane individually when its hit points are reduced to a negative number equal to or greater than its Constitution score." replace=Eidolon}

The following are new class features of the broodmaster archetype. Those with the same name as the standard summoner class have slightly different rules, but otherwise work as and replace the standard summoner class features of the same name.

::ab[Brood Link (Su)]{l=2 icon=protect ability="A broodmaster forms a close bond with his eidolons. This ability works like the standard summoner *life link* ability, except the broodmaster can only sacrifice hit points to prevent damage to one eidolon in the brood at a time. If two or more eidolons in the brood take enough damage to send them back to their home plane, the broodmaster can only sacrifice hit points to prevent damage to one of them." replace="Life link"}
::ab[Bond Senses (Su)]{icon=power l2="The broodmaster can only share the senses of one eidolon in his brood at a time."}
::ab[Shield Ally (Ex)]{icon=def l4="The broodmaster or an ally can only gain the bonuses from *shield ally* once, regardless of how many eidolons in the brood are within reach."}
::ab[Maker's Call (Su)]{icon=power l6="The broodmaster can only call one eidolon in the brood to his side with each use of this ability."}
::ab[Transposition (Su)]{icon=magic l8="The broodmaster swaps his location with that of one eidolon in the brood with each use of this ability."}

::ab[Larger Brood]{icon=boost l8="The broodmaster can spend 4 evolution points from the evolution pool for the ‹evolution/large› evolution; unlike with other evolutions, he spends these 4 points before he assigns evolution points to his individual eidolons (for an 8th-level broodmaster with 11 evolution points, this leaves 7 evolution points to distribute among the eidolons). This allows him to summon two Medium eidolons, four Small eidolons, or one Medium eidolon and two Small eidolons." l13="If the broodmaster has purchased the large evolution for his brood, he can spend an additional 6 evolution points from the evolution pool for the 'huge' option of the large evolution, distributing the remaining points among his eidolons. This allows him to call two Large eidolons, four Medium eidolons, eight Small eidolons, or any similar combination in which two smaller eidolons count as one eidolon of one size larger."}
::ab[Greater Shield Ally (Su)]{icon=protect l12="The broodmaster or an ally can only gain the bonuses from *greater shield ally,* regardless of how many eidolons in the brood are within reach."}

::ab[Brood Bond (Su)]{icon=def l14="A summoner's life becomes linked to his brood. This functions as the *life bond* ability, except the broodmaster can only transfer damage to one eidolon in the brood at a time. If that eidolon takes enough damage to send it back to its home plane, all excess damage remains with the broodmaster, killing him." replace="Life bond"}
::ab[Merge Forms (Su)]{icon=def-power l16="The broodmaster can only merge with one eidolon in the brood at a time." replace="Merge forms"}
### Hoarders of Summoned Monsters

The broodmaster and ‹arc-summoner/master summoner› archetypes can potentially have 5, 10, or even more summoned creatures in play. This is a deliberate feature of these archetypes, and means that the summoner can potentially be a strong candidate for "solo" adventuring by one player. However, in the hands of an inexperienced or moderately experienced player, dealing with all of those creatures in combat can take a long time, forcing other players to wait extended periods between their turns in the initiative. It is strongly recommended that GMs only allow these archetypes for experienced players, or decide on a way to speed up the summoner's turn (such as by allowing other players to control some of the summoned monsters).

counter_summoner.name: Counter-Summoner
description: ## Counter-Summoner
‹SOURCE Monster Summoner's Handbook/8›

While most summoners excel at conjuring monsters, there are those who focus instead on disrupting the conjurations of opponents. The counter-summoner harnesses powerful abjuration magic to instantly thwart the conjuration and summoning abilities of other casters, though at the cost of weakening his own powers of conjuration.

::ab[Counter-Summon (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic useMod=Charisma3 ability="A counter-summoner can attempt to counter a conjuration spell cast by another creature. He must identify the spell being cast as normal for ‹rule/counterspell«ing<s›. If he does so, he can attempt to counter the spell as a @HLswift or @HLimmediate_action. To counter the spell, the counter-summoner must attempt a dispel check as if using ‹spell/dispel magic›. If the spell being countered is a ‹spell/summon monster› or ‹spell/summon nature's ally› spell, the counter-summoner gains a +5 bonus on the dispel check." replace="Summon monster"}

::ab[Detect Summons (Su)]{l=2 icon=power swift="A counter-summoner can target a single creature he can see and determine if it has been summoned to its current plane from another. This ability reveals whether the target has been conjured by a conjuration (summoning) or (calling) spell, and allows the counter-summoner to attempt a Spellcraft check to identify the spell that conjured it (using the same DC as if the counter-summoner had witnessed the spell being cast)." replace="Bond senses"}

::ab[Weaken Summons (Su)]{l=10 icon=magic-lower standard="A counter-summoner can attempt to weaken a summoned or called creature within 60 feet. The targeted creature takes a -2 penalty on attack and damage rolls and to Armor Class for 1 minute per summoner level. A successful Will save (DC = 10 1/2 the counter-summoner's class level his Charisma modifier) negates this effect. A given summoned creature can't be targeted by this ability more than once per 24-hour period." replace=Aspect}
::ab[Improved Weaken Summons (Su)]{l=18 icon=lower ability="The penalty for the counter-summoner's *weaken summons* ability changes to -4." replace="Greater aspect"}

evolutionist.name: Evolutionist
description: ## Evolutionist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/79›

Changing an eidolon's evolutions is normally a slow process. As a summoner progresses in levels, his control over his eidolon's abilities increases. But an evolutionist possesses greater power over his eidolon's form, and is able to evolve his eidolon and its abilities to meet any challenge or face any threat as it comes up, seemingly on a whim.

::ab[Mutate Eidolon (Su)]{l=6 icon=power ability="An evolutionist can change his eidolon's ‹ability/evolutions› as if he had gained a level. To mutate his eidolon, the evolutionist must perform an arcane ritual that requires 24 hours of uninterrupted concentration and costs 200 gp per summoner level in material components." replace="Maker's call"}

::ab[Evolve Base Form (Su)]{l=8 icon=power ability="Whenever he gains a level, the evolutionist can change his eidolon's base form to a new base form. Once chosen, this base form is set until the evolutionist gains another summoner level." replace=Transposition}

::ab[Transmogrify (Sp)]{l=12 icon=magic ability="The evolutionist can cast ‹spell/transmogrify› as a spell-like ability once per day without the need for a material component. This ability has a casting time of 1 minute." replace="Greater shield ally"}

first_worlder.name: First Worlder
description: ## First Worlder
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Magic/35›

Most summoners have a close connection to creatures from the farthest reaches of the planes. A few have a primal connection to nature, and their power is tied to the First World. It is not unusual for these summoners to associate with druids, for like druids, first worlders have a powerful tie to the natural world. Some first worlders are driven half-mad by the strange energies and intelligences that seep into their minds and bodies, though, and these poor souls are prone to lash out at loggers, druids, or innocent passers-by without considering whether or not they are threats. A first worlder's ‹sidekick/eidolon› usually resembles a fey creature or a plant monster, though some are fantastic otherworldly animals with exaggerated features. A first worlder has the following class features.

::ab[Summon Nature's Ally (Sp)]{l=1 icon=learn-magic usage="A first worlder can cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› a number of times per day equal to 3 + his Charisma modifier." ability="At levels where a summoner would gain a more powerful *summon monster* spell as a spell-like ability, he instead gains the equivalent *summon nature's ally* spell (at 19th level, he can use ‹spell/summon nature's ally IX› or ‹spell/gate›)." special="When a first worlder gains a *summon nature's ally* spell as a spell-like ability, he adds it to his class spell list (he must still select it as a spell known if he wants to cast it as an actual spell)." replace="Summon monster"}
;;;ab{l=3 title="Fey Summons (Su)" action=Ability icon=magic}
A first worlder adds the following creatures to the lists of what he can summon with his *summon nature's ally* spell-like ability:
:::block
::row[*Summon nature's ally II*]{info="Gremlin (‹monster/jinkin›, ‹monster/pugwampi›, or ‹monster/vexgit›)."}
::row[*Summon nature's ally III*]{info="Gremlin (‹monster/nuglub›)."}
::row[*Summon nature's ally IV*]{info="‹monster/Unicorn›."}
::row[*Summon nature's ally V*]{info="‹monster/Pixie›, ‹monster/satyr›."}
::row[*Summon nature's ally VII*]{info="‹monster/Nymph›."}
;;;ab{title="Eidolon" action=Info icon=power replace="Eidolon"}
Instead of an outsider, a first worlder's eidolon has the ‹type/fey› creature type and the ‹subtype/extraplanar› subtype. Its statistics are changed from a standard eidolon as follows:
:::block
::row[Hit Dice]{info="d6 Hit Die (instead of d10)."}
::row[BAB]{info="Equal to 1/2 the eidolon's Hit Dice."}
::row[Good/Bad Saves]{info="The eidolon's ‹misc/good saves› area always Reflex and Will."}

::row[Skills]{info="The eidolon's class skills are ‹skill/Acrobatics›, ‹skill/Bluff›, ‹skill/Climb›, ‹skill/Craft›, ‹skill/Diplomacy›, ‹skill/Disguise›, ‹skill/Escape Artist›, ‹skill/Fly›, ‹skill/Knowledge (geography)›, ‹skill/Knowledge (nature)›, ‹skill/Perception›, ‹skill/Perform›, ‹skill/Sense Motive›, ‹skill/Sleight of Hand›, ‹skill/Stealth›, ‹skill/Swim›, and ‹skill/Use Magic Device›."}
::row[Senses]{info="The eidolon gains low-light vision instead of darkvision. The summoner can grant the eidolon darkvision 60 ft. as a 1-point evolution."}
::row[Damage Reduction]{info="If the first worlder chooses ‹evolution/damage_reduction>«this evolution›, he may select DR/cold iron instead of one of the alignment-based types of damage reduction."}

god_caller.name: God Caller
description: ## God Caller
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/40›

God callers follow a fractured tradition that originated in the now-lost nation of Sarkoris. They speak to an assortment of ancient divine powers to lead their communities and sometimes stir up reformations in faiths with ideals similar to their personal convictions.
::cskill{gain=diplo~intm lose=kd~ke~kg~kna~umd}

::ab[Guidance (Sp)]{icon=magic-down flavor="A god caller's eidolon has a tiny spark of divinity." ability="The eidolon can use ‹spell/guidance› as a spell-like ability at will. However, the eidolon and the god caller must remain within 50 feet of one another for the eidolon to remain at full strength. If the eidolon is beyond 50 feet but closer than 1,000 feet, its current and maximum hit point totals are reduced by half." alter="Life link"}

::ab[Divine Word (Ex)]{l=8 icon=roll-boost order=pa passive="If a god caller's eidolon would grant him a bonus with the ‹rule/aid another› action on a Diplomacy or Intimidate check, the god caller can instead choose to roll twice and take the better result. He makes the choice before rolling." ability="Once per day while his eidolon uses the aid another action to assist him in such a check, if the god caller would improve a target's attitude toward him, or gain a success if using the individual ‹rule/influence« rules›, he instead improves that target's attitude by 2 steps or it counts as 2 successes." replace=Transposition}

::ab[Divine Awareness (Sp)]{l=10 icon=magic useInc=summoner~4~10 useF="10~1~4" ability="After 10 minutes of concentration, a god caller's eidolon can see from afar as per ‹spell/clairaudience/clairvoyance›." replace=Aspect}

::ab[Divine Might (Ex)]{l=18 icon=boost passive="A god caller's eidolon's attacks are treated as epic for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction and harming mythic creatures." replace="Greater aspect"}

::ab[Overwhelming Presence (Sp)]{l=20 icon=magic ability="A god caller's eidolon can use ‹spell/overwhelming presence› three times per day. The save DC is equal to 19 + the eidolon's Charisma modifier." replace="Twin eidolon"}

leshy_caller.name: Leshy Caller
description: ## Leshy Caller
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/94›
A leshy caller summons creatures from the First World and forges a bond with a leshy eidolon.

::ab[Leshy Eidolon]{icon=info info="When a leshy caller summons her eidolon, the eidolon's body is created from extraplanar plant material and imbued with the intelligence of a leshy spirit. Its statistics are changed from those of a standard eidolon as shown below."}
::ab[Type]{icon=info info="The eidolon is still an outsider, and its statistics don't change, but it also counts as a plant. As it isn't truly a plant, it doesn't gain the plant immunities."}
;;;ab{head title="Base Form" icon=info action=Info alter=Eidolon}

The leshy caller chooses one of the base forms listed below for her eidolon. When the eidolon is summoned in an environment matching one of these base forms, the leshy caller can change the eidolon's base form to the matching form by sacrificing one daily use of her *summon nature's ally* ability as a @HL[free action].
Plant eidolon base forms are as follows.
:::block{title="Cactus (Desert)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="30 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+2 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (good), Will (bad)"}
::row[Attack]{info="sting (1d4)"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 14, Dex 14, Con 13, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/limbs› (arms), limbs (legs), ‹evolution/sting›"}
:::block{title="Conifer (Forest, Mountain)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="30 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+2 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (bad), Will (good)"}
::row[Attack]{info="2 claws (1d4)"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 14, Dex 12, Con 15, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/claws›, ‹evolution/limbs› (arms), limbs (legs)"}
:::block{title="Fungus (Swamp, Underground)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="30 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+2 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (good), Will (bad)"}
::row[Attack]{info="bite (1d6)"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 14, Dex 14, Con 13, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/bite›, ‹evolution/limbs› (arms), limbs (legs)"}
:::block{title="Leaf (Forest, Jungle, Swamp)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="30 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+2 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (good), Will (bad)"}
::row[Attack]{info="slam (1d8)"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 16, Dex 12, Con 13, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/limbs› (arms), limbs (legs), ‹evolution/slam›"}
:::block{title="Seaweed (Aquatic)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="20 ft., swim 40 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+2 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (good), Will (bad)"}
::row[Attack]{info="slam (1d8)"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 12, Dex 14, Con 15, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/limbs› (arms), ‹evolution/slam›, ‹evolution/swim› (2)"}

::ab[Summon Nature's Ally (Sp)]{icon=magic ability="A leshy caller can cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› as a spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to 3 + her Charisma modifier." info="At levels where a regular summoner would gain access to a new level of *summon monster* as a spell-like ability, the leshy caller instead gains the equivalent *summon nature's ally* spell; at 19th level, she can cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally IX› or ‹spell/gate›." passive="When a leshy caller gains a *summon nature's ally* spell as a spell-like ability, she adds it to her class spell list (though she must still select it as a spell known if she wants to gain the ability to cast it as a normal spell). She adds ‹monster/leaf leshy«s›, ‹monster/gourd leshy«s›, ‹monster/fungus leshy«s›, ‹monster/seaweed leshy«s›, and ‹monster/lotus leshy«s› to her list of creatures for *summon nature's ally I, II, III, IV,* and *V,* respectively." order=anp alter="Summon monster"}

master_summoner.name: Master Summoner
description: ## Master Summoner
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/80›

Most summoners forge a bond with a single, powerful eidolon, but some summoners seek to control a variety of creatures. The master summoner sacrifices the power of his eidolon in favor of summoning a plethora of otherworldly creatures to aid him.

::ab[Lesser Eidolon]{icon=down info="A master summoner's class level is halved (minimum 1) for the purposes of determining his ‹sidekick/eidolon«'s› abilities, Hit Dice, evolution pool, and so on. The eidolon otherwise functions as normal." replace=Eidolon}

::ab[Summoning Mastery (Sp)]{l=1 icon=magic-boost ability="A master summoner can cast ‹spell/summon monster I› as a spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to 5 + his Charisma modifier. The summoner can use this ability when his eidolon is summoned." special="Only one *summon monster* spell may be in effect while the eidolon is summoned. If the summoner's eidolon is not summoned, the number of creatures that can be summoned with this ability is only limited by its uses per day. This ability otherwise functions as the summoner's normal *summon monster I* ability. Other than these restrictions, there is no limit to how many *summon monster* or ‹spell/gate› spells the summoner can have active at one time." replace="Summon monster I~shield ally"}
::ab[Augment Summoning]{l=2 icon=power ability="A master summoner gains ‹feat/Augment Summoning› as a bonus feat. He does not have to meet any requirements for this feat." replace="Bond senses"}
### Hordes of Summoned Monsters

The ‹arc-summoner/broodmaster› and master summoner archetypes can potentially have 5, 10, or even more summoned creatures in play. This is a deliberate feature of these archetypes, and means that the summoner can potentially be a strong candidate for "solo" adventuring by one player. However, in the hands of an inexperienced or moderately experienced player, dealing with all of those creatures in combat can take a long time, forcing other players to wait extended periods between their turns in the initiative. It is strongly recommended that GMs only allow these archetypes for experienced players, or decide on a way to speed up the summoner's turn (such as by allowing other players to control some of the summoned monsters).

morphic_savant.name: Morphic Savant
description: ## Morphic Savant
‹SOURCE Monster Summoner's Handbook/16›

A morphic savant is an agent of change who draws power from the Abyss, Elysium, or the Maelstrom to fulfill whatever goals strike his fancy. Chaotic evil and chaotic neutral morphic savants often train with ‹pclass/Blackfire Adept«s› in the hopes of learning greater powers of chaotic unmaking, and chaotic good morphic savants are most common as agents of the ‹rule/Bellflower Network›.
::prereq{other="Chaotic alignment"}

::ab[Eidolon of Chaos]{icon=info-power ability="A morphic savant's eidolon must be of a chaotic alignment. The morphic savant's eidolon has three base forms: biped, quadruped, and serpentine. Each form has the same feats and skills, but has its own set of evolutions. When the summoner meditates and regains his spell slots for the day, he can select any of the three base forms of his eidolon. The eidolon has 1 fewer evolution point than normal for an eidolon of the morphic savant's summoner level, and has 1 fewer skill point per Hit Die." alter=Eidolon}

:::ab{icon=info-magic-down-boost l=1 action=Info title="Morphic Monsters" imp2="The morphic savant can grant one 1-point ‹ability/evolution<s› to all the creatures he summons with his *summon monster* spell-like ability. He can't grant an evolution that duplicates the function of, or has the same name as, any ability the summoned creatures already possess (for example, he can't grant a creature with a bite attack another bite attack), nor can he add an evolution that causes the summoned monsters to have more attacks than his eidolon's maximum number of attacks. The creatures do not have to be of the correct subtype to gain an evolution, but do have to meet any other prerequisites." alter="Summon monster"}

Since a morphic savant's power is drawn from planar energies aligned with chaos, all the creatures he summons must be of a chaotic alignment. If a creature would normally be celestial or fiendish, it is instead an ‹template/entropic« creature›.

This also causes the morphic savant's summoning power to be less reliable than a standard summoner's. Each time he uses his *summon monster* spell-like ability to summon multiple creatures, there is a 50% chance he summons one more monster than normal for the *summon monster* spell he uses, in which case the creatures remain for only 1 round per level (instead of 1 minute per level).

::ab[Chaos Magic]{icon=learn info="A morphic savant gains the following bonus spells known at the listed spell level. The morphic savant gains one fewer spell known per spell level for spell levels 1-6." s1="Protection from law" s2="Shard of chaos" s3="Magic circle against law" s4="Chaos hammer" s5="Dispel law" s6="Word of chaos" alter="Spells known"}

naturalist.name: Naturalist
description: ## Naturalist
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/123›

A naturalist is a summoner who is in tune with the natural world, using his magic like a lens to focus various animal aspects onto his eidolon. More akin to a hunter than to other arcane spellcasters, a naturalist instinctively masters the power of such creatures as the bear, wolf, mouse, or tiger to make his exotic eidolon the perfect living tool for battle or stealth, and he eventually discovers how to apply these transformations to himself as well.

::ab[Nature's Call (Sp)]{l=1 icon=magic ability="The naturalist can cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› as a spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to 3 + his Charisma modifier. He can only use this ability to summon creatures of the animal, magical beast, or vermin type. This ability otherwise functions as the *summon monster I* class feature (increasing in level as the naturalist goes up in level and with the same restrictions)." replace="Summon monster I"}

::ab[Animal Focus (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost swift="A naturalist can enhance his eidolon with the aspect of an animal. Each time he uses this ability, he can select a hunter's ‹ability/animal «aspect<focus› and apply it to his eidolon. His hunter level for this ability is equal to his summoner level - 2. He does not gain the ability to add an animal aspect to himself (see *Shared Focus,* below). This effect lasts until the eidolon is dismissed or sent back to its home plane.~~~When an animal aspect is applied to an eidolon, its form is altered by superficial physical changes appropriate to that aspect. For example, using aspect of the ‹aspect/bat› might give the eidolon larger ears and eyes and perhaps membranes of skin connecting its limbs to its body, while using aspect of the ‹aspect/stag› might give the eidolon antlers and hoof-like nails on its feat. None of these changes interfere with any of the eidolon's natural or magical abilities." replace="Shield ally~greater shield ally"}

::ab[Second Animal Focus (Su)]{l=10 icon=boost ability="Whenever a naturalist uses *animal focus,* he may apply two different animal aspects to his eidolon. The eidolon's form gains superficial physical charges appropriate to the chosen aspect." replace="Aspect"}

::ab[Shared Focus (Su)]{l=14 icon=boost flavor="The naturalist begins to take on some of the feral nature of his eidolon." ability="Whenever the naturalist uses *animal focus* to grant an aspect to his eidolon, he also gains the effects of the chosen aspect. The naturalist keeps this aspect until his eidolon is dismissed or sent back to its home plane. The naturalist gains the effects of only one aspect, even if the eidolon gains more than one (naturalist's choice)." replace="Life bond"}

::ab[Third Animal Focus (Su)]{l=18 icon=boost ability="Whenever a naturalist uses his *animal focus* ability, he can apply three different animal aspects to his eidolon (one of which lasts until he decides to change it)." replace="Greater aspect"}

pyroclast.name: Pyroclast
description: ## Pyroclast
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #95: Anvil of Fire/72›
Pyroclasts harness the primal elemental energy of magma. Their eidolons often resemble elemental beings.

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down info="A pyroclast receives one fewer spell per day of each level than normal. If this reduces the number of spells for a level to 0, he can cast spells of that level only if his Charisma score grants him ‹rule/bonus spells› of that level." alter=Spells}

::ab[Bound to Fire (Ex)]{icon=def-down passive="A pyroclast's eidolon begins with ‹umr/resist› fire 10 and ‹umr/vulnerability› to cold." imp5="This improves to resist fire 20." imp10="A pyroclast's eidolon gains the ‹subtype/fire› subtype and ‹umr/immunity› to fire." special="This ability permanently reduces the eidolon's evolution pool by 1 point."}

::ab[Detonate (Su)]{l=6 icon=aura ability="A pyroclast can banish his eidolon in an explosion of fiery shards. This slays the eidolon and deals 1d6 points of damage per Hit Die of the eidolon to every creature in a 20-foot-radius ‹misc/burst› (Reflex half, DC = 10 + 1/2 his summoner level + his Charisma modifier). Half of this damage is fire, and the other half is piercing." imp12="The pyroclast can reduce the damage by 4d6 to increase the burst radius to 40 feet." usage="Once per day" replace="Maker's call"}

::ab[Heart of Earth and Fire (Ex)]{l=8 icon=boost-def passive="A pyroclast grants any of his summoned creatures with the earth or fire subtypes a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls, saving throws, and weapon damage rolls when he is within 30 feet." imp10="The pyroclast also grants these bonuses to his eidolon." replace=Transposition}

shadow_caller.name: Shadow Caller
description: ## Shadow Caller
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/110›

While most summoners can call any manner of creature from across the planes to serve them in combat, supplementing the skills of their eidolon with a diverse range of creatures, others eschew this broad utility and instead concentrate upon calling forth entities from the ‹rule/Shadow Plane›. A shadow caller has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Fetchling}
::cskill{gain=stl lose=umd}
;;;ab{title="Shadow Summoning (Sp)" icon=magic action=Ability replace=" summon monster spell-like"}

When a shadow caller uses his *summon monster* ability or casts the ‹spell/summon monster› spell, he typically summons creatures from the Shadow Plane or creatures closely associated with shadow. When a creature on the *summon monster* spell list indicates that it is summoned with either the celestial or the fiendish template based on the alignment of the caster, the creature summoned by the shadow caller has the ‹template/shadow› creature template instead. Furthermore, the *summon monster* lists are modified in the following ways (these changes also apply to using a higher-level *summon* spell to summon multiple creatures from a lower-level list).
:::block
::row[*Summon Monster I*]{info="No changes."}
::row[*Summon Monster II*]{info="A shadow caller cannot summon Small elementals or lemures, but instead can summon ‹monster/zoog«s›."}
::row[*Summon Monster III*]{info="A shadow caller cannot summon dretches or lantern archons, but can summon ‹monster/augur kyton«s›."}

::row[*Summon Monster IV*]{info="A shadow caller cannot summon Medium elementals, hell hounds, hound archons, or mephits, but can summon ‹monster/allip«s›, ‹monster/gloomwing«s›, and ‹monster/shadow«s›."}

::row[*Summon Monster V*]{info="A shadow caller cannot summon babau, bearded devils, bralani azatas, Large elementals, salamanders, or xills, but can summon ‹monster/shadow mastiff«s› and ‹monster/shae›."}
::row[*Summon Monster VI*]{info="A shadow caller cannot summon Huge elementals, erinyes, lillend azatas, or succubi, but can summon ‹monster/cloaker«s›."}
::row[*Summon Monster VII*]{info="A shadow caller cannot summon bebiliths, bone devils, greater elementals, or vrocks, but can summon ‹monster/greater shadow«s›."}
::row[*Summon Monster VIII*]{info="A shadow caller can only summon ‹monster/derghodaemon«s› and young ‹family/umbral dragon«s›."}
::row[*Summon Monster IX*]{info="A shadow caller can only summon ‹monster/interlocutor kyton«s› and ‹monster/nightwing«s›."}

::ab[Shadow Eidolon]{icon=info-magic info="A shadow caller's eidolon is at once a thing of shadow called from the deep of the Shadow Plane and his own shadow; the two are inseparable. When his eidolon manifests, his shadow lengthens and finally detaches from him as a creature unto itself. For as long as the shadow caller's eidolon is manifested, he and the eidolon do not have distinct shadows, regardless of the presence or absence of light." replace="Magical symbol that identifies the summoner and his eidolon" alter=Eidolon}

shaitan_binder.name: Shaitan Binder
description: ## Shaitan Binder
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/146›
Shaitan binders call upon a reflection of their genie ancestors to serve as their eidolons. A shaitan binder has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Oread}

::ab[Base Form]{l=1 icon=boost-down passive="If a shaitan binder's eidolon has the biped base form, it gains a +2 bonus to one ability score. The shaitan binder must make this choice at 1st level. If at any time the shaitan binder's eidolon has another base form, it loses this bonus until it returns to biped form." special="A shaitan binder's eidolon does not gain the *share spells* ability."}

::ab[Shaitan Magic (Su)]{icon=power l4="A shaitan binder's eidolon gains the ‹evolution/basic magic› evolution as a free evolution." l6="It gains the ‹evolution/minor magic› evolution." l8="It gains the ‹evolution/major magic› evolution as a free evolution, and adds the following to the list of available spells for that evolution: ‹spell/glitterdust› and ‹spell/soften earth and stone›." l12="It gains the ‹evolution/ultimate magic› evolution as a free evolution, and adds the following to the list of available spells for that evolution: ‹spell/meld into stone› and ‹spell/stone shape›." special="Although the shaitan binder gains the standard versions of these evolutions for free, he must pay the normal cost to upgrade them to the improved versions." replace="Shield ally~greater shield ally"}

::ab[Earth Glide (Su)]{l=10 icon=power ability="If a shaitan binder's eidolon has the ‹evolution/burrow› evolution, it gains the ‹umr/earth glide› universal monster ability and can use this ability to travel at its full base speed." replace=Aspect}

::ab[Stone Curse (Su)]{l=18 head icon=power ability="A shaitan binder may select *stone curse* as a 4-point evolution. This allows the eidolon to trap creatures in stone like the ‹monster/shaitan› *stone curse* ability. The DC to resist or break free of the *stone curse* is 10 + 1/2 the eidolon's Hit Dice + the eidolon's Strength score." replace="Greater aspect"}

::ab[Stone Curse (Su)]{next icon=melee flavor="Text from the shaitan ability." ability="If a shaitan wins a ‹rule/bull rush› check by 5 or more and pushes its target into a stone barrier, the target must make a Reflex save or be forced into the barrier as if the target had cast *meld into stone* until the victim makes a successful Fortitude save as a @HLfull-round_action to exit the stone."}

::ab[Noble Eidolon (Sp)]{l=20 icon=magic ability="A shaitan binder's eidolon gains the ability to grant its summoner's wishes. Once per day, the eidolon can cast ‹spell/limited wish› as a spell-like ability. The eidolon's caster level is equal to its level. The wish must be spoken aloud by the shaitan binder, beginning with the words &quot&I wish,&quot& and cannot duplicate a wish the eidolon has granted within the past 24 hours. If the eidolon uses this ability to duplicate a spell with a costly material component, the shaitan binder must provide that component." replace="Twin eidolon"}

spirit_summoner.name: Spirit Summoner
description: ## Spirit Summoner
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/123›

A spirit summoner is an arcane spellcaster whose eidolon is a manifestation of a shamanic spirit. The bond connecting the summoner and this spirit might be friendly, as a member of a tribe giving flesh to his protector spirit, or hostile, as an outlander or rival compelling service from an unwilling spirit. The summoner can draw upon the divine power of this spirit, but is not constrained by the spirit's morals or ethics.

::ab[Spirit (Su)]{icon=power-learn ability="A spirit summoner forms a mystical bond with one ‹ability/shaman_>spirit<s› from the shaman class. He gains the *spirit ability* of that spirit and uses his summoner level as his shaman level for determining its effects. The spirit summoner can select spells granted by that spirit as summoner spells known, using the shaman level of the spell as the summoner spell level. The summoner cannot select spirit spells that are above 6th level." imp9="The summoner gains the abilities listed in the *greater* version of his selected spirit." imp17="The summoner gains the abilities listed for the *true* version of his selected spirit." replace="Summon monster"}

::ab[Eidolon]{l=1 icon=power-down info="A spirit summoner begins play with a powerful outsider called an eidolon. The eidolon's form and abilities must be appropriate to his chosen spirit, at the GM's discretion - for example, a summoner with the ‹shamanspirit/flame› spirit cannot have an eidolon with ice or water abilities, a summoner with a ‹shamanspirit/heavens› spirit cannot have a fiend-like eidolon, and so on. The summoner can use temporary effects (such as *evolution surge*) to alter the eidolon into an inappropriate form, but cannot use permanent effects (such as *transmogrify*) to do so." alter=Eidolon}

::ab[Hex]{l=6 icon=power ability="A spirit summoner can select a shaman hex that is appropriate to his spirit, chosen from the general list of ‹ability/shaman hexes› or the specific hexes allowed by his spirit. This otherwise functions as a shaman's hex, and uses his summoner level as his shaman level. Like a shaman, a spirit summoner uses his Wisdom modifier to determine hex DCs." repeatAt="The summoner can select another hex.~8~10~16" replace="Aspect~maker's call~merge forms~transposition"}

storm_caller.name: Storm Caller
description: ## Storm Caller
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/16›

Many summoners beckon monsters from the Elemental Planes, but few are as specialized as storm callers, who channel the elemental energies of the Plane of Air. Storm callers are most commonly encountered in areas where thunderstorms appear frequently, including supernaturally stormy places like the Sodden Lands.

::ab[Stormy Eidolon]{icon=warning info="A storm caller must select the ‹evolution/resistance› (electricity) or ‹evolution/immunity› (electricity) evolution for his eidolon. If the eidolon has neither of these evolutions, it becomes ‹misc/nauseated› until it regains one of them." alter=Eidolon}
;;;ab{title="Summon Storm's Fury (Sp)" action=Ability icon=down-magic-learn alter="Summon monster I-IX"}

A storm caller can summon creatures infused with the power of the storm to help him. This functions as the summoner's *summon monster I-IX* abilities, except the storm caller can summon only the following creatures at the indicated spell levels:
:::block{size=tiny}
::row[I]{info="‹monster/eagle›"}
::row[II]{info="‹monster/small air elemental›, ‹monster/small lightning elemental›"}
::row[III]{info="‹monster/dire bat›"}
::row[IV]{info="‹monster/medium air elemental›, ‹monster/medium lightning elemental›, ‹monster/pteranodon›"}
::row[V]{info="‹monster/large air elemental›, ‹monster/large lightning elemental›"}
::row[VI]{info="‹monster/huge air elemental›, ‹monster/huge lightning elemental›"}
::row[VII]{info="‹monster/roc›"}
::row[VIII]{info="‹monster/elder air elemental›, ‹monster/elder lightning elemental›"}
::row[IX]{info="‹template/advanced› elder air elemental, advanced elder lightning elemental"}
Any creatures summoned with this ability gain immunity to electricity.

Alternatively, whenever his eidolon is not summoned, a storm caller can expend a daily use of this ability to summon a magical storm as a @HL[standard action]. This functions as ‹spell/call lightning› with a caster level equal to his summoner level, except as noted below.

Each bolt of lightning deals 1d6 points of electricity damage. As the summoner gains levels, this damage increases as indicated on the table below. The prevailing weather has no effect on the damage of the lightning bolts. The Reflex save DC is equal to 10 + half the storm caller's summoner level + his Charisma modifier. If the storm caller uses this ability again before he has used all of his available bolts of lightning (to summon another magical storm, to summon a monster, or to use the ‹spell/gate› spell), any unused bolts are expended with no effect.
| Level | Damage |

The storm caller adds *call lightning* to his summoner spell list as a 3rd-level spell and ‹spell/call lightning storm› to his summoner spell list as a 5th-level spell, but he does not automatically add them to his list of spells known.

::ab[Electrical Polarity (Su)]{l=4 icon=line ability="A storm caller and his eidolon can temporarily form an electrical current between them. The storm caller can expend a use of his *summon storm's fury* ability as a @HLstandard_action to create an instantaneous line of lightning between him and his eidolon, provided that the eidolon is within 60 feet of him. The lightning arc deals the same amount of damage to all creatures in the line and has the same save DC as a bolt of lightning from the *summon storm's fury* ability (unlike *call lightning,* this damage is dealt only once). The storm caller and his eidolon are not affected by this lightning." imp12="The storm caller gains the benefit of his eidolon's resistance (electricity) or immunity (electricity) evolution whenever he is within 30 feet of the eidolon." replace="Shield ally~greater shield ally"}

::ab[Storm's Wings (Su)]{l=6 icon=power ability="A storm caller can borrow some of his eidolon's elemental might. He can gain the ‹evolution/flight› evolution as though he were using the summoner's *aspect* ability, deducting 2 evolution points from the eidolon's evolution pool. If he does so, his eidolon is unable to take the flight evolution as long as the storm caller has that evolution." imp8="The storm caller's eidolon can have the flight evolution even if the storm caller has that evolution." imp10="The storm caller automatically gains the flight evolution without reducing the number of evolution points available to the eidolon. He can use the *aspect* ability (and at 18th level, the *greater aspect* ability) to gain additional evolutions." replace="Maker's call~transposition"}

story_summoner.name: Story Summoner
description: ## Story Summoner
‹SOURCE The Harrow Handbook/25›

Story summoners rely on the harrow to weave the paths they walk, turning their tales into reality. They channel magic through their harrow decks to influence the strange extraplanar beings they summon, bringing familiar and fateful characters to life. Some focus on the more whimsical figures of the harrow, while others bring its darkest icons into the real world.

::ab[Storykin Eidolon]{icon=boost-down order=usn info="Unlike most eidolons, a story summoner's eidolon cannot touch or attack creatures warded by ‹spell/protection from evil› and similar effects that prevent contact with summoned creatures." standard="While his eidolon is summoned, the story summoner can draw a random card from a complete ‹eq-misc/harrow deck› he owns. For a number of rounds equal to 1/2 the summoner's class level (minimum 1), the eidolon's alignment changes to match that of the harrow card drawn, and the eidolon gains a +4 enhancement bonus on the ability score linked to the drawn card's suit. The eidolon continues to follow its summoner's orders regardless of its alignment." useMod=Charisma3}

::ab[Summon Arcana]{l=2 icon=boost ability="A story summoner can use a complete harrow deck he owns as an additional focus component for his *summon monster* spell-like ability. When he does so, the summoner draws a random card from the deck and applies a simple template to the summoned monster based on the card's alignment: ‹template/celestial› (any good), ‹template/fiendish› (any evil), ‹template/resolute› (any lawful), or ‹template/entropic› (any chaotic). If a card has more than one alignment - such as a lawful evil or chaotic good card - the summoner can choose which template to apply to the summoned creature." special="This ability allows a story summoner to summon and command creatures normally prohibited by their alignment." replace="Bond senses"}

::ab[Evolve Base Form (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic ability="Whenever he gains a level, the story summoner can change his eidolon's base form to a new base form. Once chosen, this base form is set until the story summoner gains another summoner level." replace=Transposition}

synthesist.name: Synthesist
description: ## Synthesist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/81›

Rather than summon an eidolon to serve by his side, the synthesist fuses his eidolon's essence to his own. Instead of two creatures, the synthesist is a fusion of the summoner and eidolon into a single being.
:::ab{title="Fused Eidolon" action=Ability icon=power-boost-def-down replace="Eidolon~bond senses~life bond"}

A synthesist summons the essence of a powerful outsider to meld with his own being. The synthesist wears the eidolon like translucent, living armor. The eidolon mimics all of the synthesist's movements, and the synthesist perceives through the eidolon's senses and speaks through its voice, as the two are now one creature. The synthesist directs all of the eidolon's actions while fused, perceives through its senses, and speaks through its voice, as the two are now one creature.

While fused with his eidolon, the synthesist uses the eidolon's Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution, but retains his own Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. The synthesist gains the eidolon's hit points as ‹rule/temporary hit points›. When these hit points reach 0, the eidolon is killed and sent back to its home plane. The synthesist uses the eidolon's base attack bonus, and gains the eidolon's armor and natural armor bonuses and modifiers to ability scores. The synthesist also gains access to the eidolon's special abilities and the eidolon's evolutions. The synthesist is still limited to the eidolon's maximum number of natural attacks. The eidolon has no skills or feats of its own. The eidolon must be at least the same size as the synthesist. The eidolon must have ‹evolution/limbs› for the synthesist to cast spells with somatic components. The eidolon's temporary hit points can be restored with the ‹spell/rejuvenate eidolon› spell.

While fused, the synthesist loses the benefits of his armor. He counts both as his original type and as an outsider for any effect related to type, whichever is worse for the synthesist. Spells such as ‹spell/banishment› or ‹spell/dismissal› work normally on the eidolon, but the synthesist is unaffected. Neither the synthesist nor his eidolon can be targeted separately, as they are fused into one creature. The synthesist and eidolon cannot take separate actions. While fused with his eidolon, the synthesist can use all of his own abilities and gear, except for his armor. In all other cases, this ability functions as the summoner's normal eidolon ability (for example, the synthesist cannot use his *summon monster* ability while the eidolon is present).
The following class abilities function differently for synthesist summoners.

::ab[Fused Link (Su)]{l=1 icon=protect ability="The synthesist forms a close bond with his eidolon. Whenever the temporary hit points from his eidolon would be reduced to 0, the summoner can, as a @HLfree_action, sacrifice any number of his own hit points. Each hit point sacrificed this way prevents 1 point of damage done to the eidolon (thus preventing the loss of the summoner's temporary hit points), preventing the eidolon from being killed and sent back to its home plane." replace="Life link"}

::ab[Shielded Meld (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="Whenever the synthesist is fused with his eidolon, he gains a +2 shield bonus to his Armor Class and a +2 circumstance bonus on his saving throws." replace="Shield ally"}

::ab[Maker's Jump (Sp)]{l=6 icon=magic useInc=summoner~6 useF="6~1" ability="Whenever the synthesist is fused with his eidolon, the synthesist can cast ‹spell/dimension door› as a spell-like ability using his caster level. This ability only affects the fused synthesist and eidolon." replace="Maker's call~transposition"}

::ab[Greater Shielded Meld (Ex)]{l=12 icon=def passive="Whenever the synthesist is fused with his eidolon, he gains a +4 shield bonus to his Armor Class and a +4 circumstance bonus on his saving throws." replace="Greater shield ally"}

::ab[Split Forms (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic useL=summoner useNC swift="The synthesist and his fused eidolon can split into two creatures: the synthesist and the eidolon. Both have the same evolutions. The synthesist emerges in a square adjacent to the eidolon if possible. All effects and spells currently targeting the fused synthesist-eidolon affect both the synthesist and the eidolon." special="The synthesist can end this effect at any time as a @HLfull-round_action. For the duration of this effect, the eidolon functions as a normal eidolon of the summoner's class level." replace="Merge forms"}

twinned_summoner.name: Twinned Summoner
description: ## Twinned Summoner
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/18›
Reflecting the connected nature of Imbrex, a twinned summoner and his eidolon appear identical save for any obvious differences, such as evolutions or equipment.

::ab[Twinned Eidolon]{icon=down info="The twinned summoner's eidolon is restricted to the biped base form. If the summoner's natural size is smaller than Medium, the twinned summoner's eidolon must be Small. This ability may require a different base form to match the twinned summoner's natural form, at the GM's discretion, if the twinned summoner is not a bipedal creature."}

::ab[Teamwork Feats (Ex)]{icon=power l4="A twinned summoner gains ‹main/feats>a bonus» teamwork« feat›. He must meet the prerequisites of the selected teamwork feat." l12="The summoner gains another bonus teamwork feat he meets the prerequisites of." special="The twinned summoner automatically grants all of his teamwork feats to his eidolon. The eidolon doesn't need to meet the prerequisites of these teamwork feats." replace="Shield ally~greater shield ally"}

::ab[Twinned Transposition (Su)]{l=6 icon=magic useInc=summoner~4~6 useF="6~1~4" standard="A twinned summoner can swap locations with his eidolon as if each were using ‹spell/dimension door›, using the twinned summoner's caster level. If the eidolon is out of range, the ability is wasted. If either the twinned summoner or his eidolon occupies more squares than the other, each must occupy one or more squares that were occupied by the other if possible, or as close as possible if not." imp8="He can use this ability as a @HLswift_action." replace="Maker's call~transposition"}

::ab[Twin Summoner (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic standard="A twinned summoner can touch his eidolon, enhancing its similarity to the summoner. The eidolon loses any natural weapons or size increases gained from evolutions, and its Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores change to match the base scores of the summoner. The eidolon can cast any of the summoner's spells known using the summoner's caster level and spell slots. It doesn't gain the benefit of any of the summoner's items, feats, or other effects that affect spellcasting." useL=summoner useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="The summoner can end this effect as a @HLfree_action." replace="Merge forms"}
::ab[Twinned Eidolon Evolutions]{icon=power info="The following evolutions are only available to this archetype." head}

::ab[Shared Evolution]{next xCost="1 point" order=xs standard="The eidolon can touch the summoner and transfer a 1-point or 2-point evolution to him. This functions as the summoner's *aspect* ability, and the same limitations apply." special="The summoner can touch the eidolon as a @HLstandard_action to return the evolution. The evolution returns to the eidolon automatically if the eidolon is dismissed by the summoner or sent back to its home plane." icon=boost}
::ab[Extra Feat]{next xCost="2 points" order=xa ability="The eidolon gains an extra feat. It must meet the prerequisites of the feat." icon=power}

::ab[Shared Slot]{next xCost="2 points" order=xa ability="Select a magic item slot. A magic item worn by the eidolon in that slot remains active even if the summoner is also wearing a magic item in that slot." special="This evolution can be selected more than once. Each time an eidolon selects this evolution, it applies to a new slot." icon=power}

unwavering_conduit.name: Unwavering Conduit
description: ## Unwavering Conduit
‹SOURCE Monster Summoner's Handbook/17›

Unwavering conduits work to keep everything ordered and regimented, drawing on power from the Eternal City of Axis, Heaven, or Hell to enforce whichever laws the summoner finds paramount. Lawful good unwavering conduits often work with Riftwardens to protect the integrity of the Great Beyond, while lawful evil unwavering conduits are commonly diabolists in Cheliax.
::prereq{other="Alignment must be lawful"}

::ab[Eidolon of Law]{icon=info-def-down order=np info="An unwavering conduit's eidolon must be of a lawful alignment. Once selected, the evolutions of the unwavering conduit's eidolon cannot be changed when a new class level is gained (though they can be altered normally with the ‹spell/transmogrify› spell). At least half the eidolon's skill points must be spent on skills in which the unwavering conduit also has ranks." passive="The eidolon gains a resistance bonus on saving throws against spells and abilities of the conjuration, enchantment, and transmutation schools. This bonus is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-third› of the summoner's class level." special="The eidolon has 1 fewer evolution point than normal for an eidolon of the unwavering conduit's summoner level." alter=Eidolon}

::ab[Unwavering Monsters]{icon=info-def info="Since an unwavering conduit's power is drawn from planar energies aligned with law, all the creatures he summons must be of a lawful alignment. If a creature would normally be celestial or fiendish, it is instead a ‹template/resolute« creature›." imp3="The unwavering conduit's summoning power to be more set than a standard summoner's. Each time he uses *summon monster* to summon multiple monsters, rather than rolling a die to see how many creatures he summons, he receives a specific number (two creatures for 1d3, or four creatures for 1d4+1). If an item, spell, or ability would increase the number of creatures summoned, the set number increases by that amount." imp2="Creatures summoned by the unwavering conduit receive a +1 resistance bonus on saving throws against spells and abilities of the conjuration, enchantment, and transmutation schools." increment="The resistance bonus~2~4" alter="Summon monster"}

::ab[Law Magic]{icon=learn info="The unwavering conduit gains the following bonus spells known at the listed spell level." s1="Protection from chaos" s2="Arrow of law" s3="Magic circle against chaos" s4="Order's wrath" s5="Dispel chaos" s6=Dictum special="The unwavering conduit gains one fewer spell known per spell level for spell levels 1-6." alter="Spells known"}

wild_caller_arg.name: Wild Caller (ARG)
description: ## Wild Caller (ARG)
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/44›

Often a half-elf's ties to nature and elven heritage are so strong that they can dramatically affect the nature of his summonings. The wild caller calls eidolons that take more natural and savage forms and summons from nature rather than the Great Beyond. A wild caller has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Half-elf}

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn info="A wild caller does not have access to *summon monster* spells. Instead, he swaps out those spells on his spell list with ‹spell/summon nature's ally› spells of the same level." alter="Spell list of the summoner"}
;;;ab{icon=boost-down title=Eidolon flavor="The eidolon of a wild caller tends to take more natural and more savage forms than other summoners' eidolons." action=Info alter="Eidolon"}
A wild caller gains ‹misc/one-fourth› of his class level as bonus evolution points in his eidolon's evolution pool, but his eidolon cannot take the following evolutions.
:::block

::row[1-point evolutions]{info="Basic magic, magic attacks, ‹evolution/skilled› (while the eidolon can select this evolution, it can only do so with the following skills: Acrobatics, Climb, Escape Artist, Fly, Intimidate, Perception, Survival, and Swim), unnatural aura"}
::row[2-point evolutions]{info="Energy attacks, immunity, weapon training, channel resistance, head, minor magic, undead appearance"}
::row[3-point evolutions]{info="Damage reduction, frightful presence, major magic"}
::row[4-point evolutions]{info="Breath weapon, spell resistance, dimension door, incorporeal form, lifesense, no breath, ultimate magic"}

::ab[Summon Nature's Ally I (Sp)]{l=1 icon=magic useMod=Charisma3 ability="A wild caller can cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› a number of times per day equal to 3 + his Charisma modifier."  incrementRoman="This ability improves to ‹spell/summon nature's~1~2~2" incrementDesc=ally incrementEnd="›." incrementMax=18 imp19="The wild caller cannot use *gate* as a spell-like ability, but can use either ‹spell/summon elder worm› or ‹spell/summon froghemoth› instead." special="This ability otherwise functions like the standard summoner's *summon monster I* ability." replace="Summon monster I"}

wild_caller_hotw.name: Wild Caller (HotW)
description: ## Wild Caller (HotW)
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Wild/25›
A wild caller summons creatures from the First World and forges a bond with an eidolon with a plant body.

::ab[Plant Eidolon]{icon=info info="When a wild caller summons his eidolon, the eidolon's body is created from extraplanar plant material and imbued with the intelligence of a being from the First World. Its statistics are changed from a standard eidolon as shown below."}

::ab[Type]{icon=info info="The eidolon has the ‹type/plant› creature type and ‹subtype/extraplanar› subtype. Unlike other plant creatures, the eidolon is not immune to mind-affecting or polymorph effects, though it does gain immunity to paralysis, poison, sleep effects, and stunning."}
;;;ab{head title="Base Form" icon=info action=Info alter=Eidolon}

The wild caller chooses one of the base forms listed below for his plant eidolon. When the eidolon is summoned in an environment matching one of these base forms, the wild caller can choose to change the eidolon's base form to the matching form by sacrificing one daily use of his *summon nature's ally* ability as a @HL[free action].
Plant eidolon base forms are as follows.
:::block{title="Cactus (desert)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="30 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+2 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (good), Will (bad)"}
::row[Attack]{info="slam (1d8), sting (1d4)"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 14, Dex 14, Con 13, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/limbs› (arms), limbs (legs), ‹evolution/sting›, ‹evolution/tail›"}
:::block{title="Conifer (forest, mountain)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="30 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+2 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (bad), Will (good)"}
::row[Attack]{info="2 claws (1d4)"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 14, Dex 12, Con 15, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11; Resist cold 10"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/claws›, ‹evolution/limbs› (arms), limbs (legs), ‹evolution/resistance› (cold)"}
:::block{title="Mushroom (swamp, underground)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="20 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+2 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (good), Will (bad)"}
::row[Attack]{info="bite (1d6) plus poison"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 14, Dex 14, Con 13, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/bite›, ‹evolution/limbs› (arms), limbs (legs), ‹evolution/poison›"}
:::block{title="Tree (forest, jungle, swamp)"}
::row[Size]{info=Medium}
::row[Speed]{info="20 ft."}
::row[AC]{info="+4 natural armor"}
::row[Saves]{info="Fort (good), Ref (good), Will (bad)"}
::row[Attack]{info="2 slams (1d8)"}
::row[Ability Scores]{info="Str 16, Dex 12, Con 13, Int 7, Wis 10, Cha 11"}
::row[Free Evolutions]{info="‹evolution/improved natural armor›, ‹evolution/limbs› (arms), limbs (legs), ‹evolution/slam› &times&2"}

::ab[Summon Nature's Ally (Sp)]{icon=magic ability="A wild caller can cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› as a spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to 3 + his Charisma modifier." info="At levels where a regular summoner would gain access to a new level of *summon monster* as a spell-like ability, the wild caller instead gains the equivalent *summon nature's ally* spell; at 19th level, he can use ‹spell/summon nature's ally IX› or ‹spell/gate›." passive="When a wild caller gains a *summon nature's ally* spell as a spell-like ability, he adds it to his class spell list (though he must still select it as a spell known if he wants to gain the ability to cast it as a normal spell)." order=anp alter="Summon monster"}
::ab[Fey Friend (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost passive="The wild caller gains a +4 bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive checks against fey." replace=Aspect}
::ab[Aspect]{l=18 icon=boost passive="The wild caller gains the *aspect* ability of a normal summoner." replace="Greater aspect"}
